title: Colonel Karl Nell on the evolution of life on earth & faster than light travel #space #earth #aliens

transcript.text: argument that's usually made is that
life is uncommon and that faster than
light travel is not possible both of
these things really have no evidence for
them it's the exact opposite so for
instance uh their blue green algae
fossilized in stromatolites from 3.5
billion years ago and recent scholarship
has indicated that life may have evolved
even earlier 4.1 billion years ago so
the Earth itself is 5 billion years old
the crust has not even cooled by uh 4.1
billion years ago so there's evidence
that life evolved on Earth at the
earliest possible time even earlier than
would be reasonably um suspected there's
microbes that can live in outer space
that we've determined are viable and
recently as of maybe a month ago people
identified microbes that were in stasis
in two billion year old terrestrial rock
that were brought back to life so it's
in essence life is either evolving very
quickly or some sort of pans spermia
situation going on that's what all the
evidence sort of suggest
